one_line_takeaway: FreqMark encodes a sinusoidal signal into LLM token ranks and recovers it with STFT, delivering strong sentence-level detection (F1 0.95, AUC up to 0.98) that survives ~30-40% token edits where statistical and commercial detectors fail.
key_finding: At sample level FreqMark reaches AUC 0.98 (GPT-3.5-turbo-instruct) and 0.96 (Llama-2-7b) with low FPR/FNR (0.08/0.02 and 0.10/0.04), versus the strongest baseline DetectGPT at 0.87/0.83 AUC. At sentence level, where zero-shot statistical and commercial detectors collapse, FreqMark scores Precision 0.92 / Recall 0.98 / F1 0.95 against Log p(x) (0.16/0.18/0.17) and GPTZero (0.21/0.15/0.18). The watermark stays highly detectable (AUC >= 0.9) up to roughly 30-40% token modification, with paraphrasing and substitution being the most damaging attacks (largest hit to both perplexity and AUC) and copy-pasting/translation the least.
core_contribution: FreqMark is a frequency-based watermarking scheme for LLM-generated text that (unlike prior green/red-list token watermarks aimed at sample-level binary classification) enables fine-grained, sentence/token-level detection in mixed human+LLM text. Its two novel pieces are: (1) embedding a soft watermark during the sampling process by using a periodic guiding signal (a sinusoid with amplitude 1-5) to dictate which log-probability RANK the next token is drawn from, i.e. R(w_t)=a(t mod k), producing a periodic waveform over token ranks; and (2) detecting that watermark by reconstructing the per-token rank sequence and running Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) analysis with a sliding window so the periodic frequency (~0.1 Hz) can be localized to individual segments. The authors also construct a mixed-content 'mixed-realnewslike' dataset to benchmark localization under text manipulation.
threat_model: The defender is a party wanting to trace/attribute LLM-generated text to curb misuse (disinformation/fake news, academic dishonesty). The generator embeds the watermark at sampling time; a detector who can reconstruct the token logit distribution later tests text for the periodic rank pattern. The adversary is a user who post-edits watermarked output to evade detection while keeping the text usable. Four concrete evasion attacks are modeled: copy-pasting (splicing human prompt text with watermarked text), paraphrasing (rewriting sentences while preserving meaning), substitution (replacing token spans with google-t5/t5-3b), and translation (English->Spanish->English round-trip). Detection assumes the text was generated purely under this watermark's rank-selection (no beam search / nucleus sampling mixed in) so logits can be approximated, and assumes semantic continuity of preceding context for accurate logit reconstruction.
limitations: Author-stated: degrades on very short texts (too few tokens for the periodic pattern to manifest), under aggressive paraphrasing / heavy substitution, and on highly structured low-variability text; detection accuracy drops if generation mixed in beam search or nucleus sampling (breaking the logit-reconstruction assumption), and if preceding context is semantically unrelated or spans a large contextual gap the logit prediction (and thus detection) suffers. Reviewer-observed gaps: results come from a single small realnewslike-derived dataset with no reported confidence intervals or seeds; only two generators tested; robustness is shown only as a figure (no per-attack numeric table); temperature=0 (greedy) is required for clean embedding, which constrains output diversity and may not reflect deployed sampling; and detection presumes access to (or a close proxy of) the generating model's logits, a strong practical assumption.

datasets_used: mixed-realnewslike (authors' constructed mixed human+LLM dataset built from the realnewslike/C4 corpus, following Kirchenbauer et al.)
Balanced sample-level set: equal pure human-written vs pure watermarked text
Sentence-level enhanced copy-pasting subset: mixed segments of 20-100 tokens, minimum 1:1 LLM:human ratio, per-token labels
quantitative_results: Sample-level AUC (GPT-3.5-turbo-instruct): FreqMark 0.98 vs DetectGPT 0.87, LogRank 0.69, LRR 0.66
Sample-level FreqMark on GPT-3.5-turbo-instruct: FPR 0.08, FNR 0.02
Sample-level AUC (Llama-2-7b): FreqMark 0.96 vs DetectGPT 0.83, LRR 0.70; FreqMark FPR 0.10, FNR 0.04
Sentence-level: FreqMark Precision 0.92 / Recall 0.98 / F1 0.95 vs Log p(x) 0.16/0.18/0.17 and GPTZero 0.21/0.15/0.18
Robustness: watermark remains AUC >= 0.9 up to ~30-40% token modification; paraphrasing and substitution most degrade AUC and perplexity
Watermark config: sinusoidal signal, amplitude 1-5, 10 sampling points per cycle; STFT window length 10, overlap 9, Hann window; peak frequency ~0.1 Hz
